title: How to Stop Fighting Your Tinnitus and Start Living
phrases: "The gap between stimulus and response is where your entire life lives."
"The algorithm of your attention determines the landscape of your experience."
"The most important things in life cannot be understood - only experienced."

title: Emotional Regulation Skills for Tinnitus Distress
phrases: "The breath doesn't need your management. It needs your companionship."
"Not every insight requires action. Some just need to be witnessed."
"Stop pathologizing normal human suffering. Not everything requires a diagnosis."
interjections: Stick with this for a moment.

title: The Window of Tolerance and Tinnitus Reactivity
phrases: "The self you're trying to improve is the same self doing the improving. Notice the circularity."
"Stillness is not something you achieve. It's what's already here beneath the achieving."
"The body has a grammar. Most of us never learned to read it."
"The algorithm of your attention determines the landscape of your experience."
interjections: Let that land for a second.
And this is the part nobody talks about.

title: How Journaling Helps Process Tinnitus Emotions
phrases: "The paradox of acceptance is that nothing changes until you stop demanding that it does."
"The body remembers what the mind would prefer to file away."
"Attention is the most undervalued resource you have. Everything else follows from where you place it."
"The nervous system doesn't respond to what you believe. It responds to what it senses."
"Sit with it long enough and even the worst feeling reveals its edges."
researchers: Josef Rauschecker (Georgetown neuroscientist, tinnitus brain mapping)

phrases: "Every resistance is information. The question is whether you're willing to read it."
